middleware.description: The core request-boundary capture. Reads cookies (`_utm`, `_gclid`, `_fbclid`, `_ttclid`, `_msclkid`, `_sccid`, `_session_id`) + query params + Referer header + User-Agent. Runs `ConsentGate::allows('marketing')` — when granted: creates or updates the attributions row + appends an attribution_touchpoints row. When NOT granted: captures ONLY session_id + hashed_ip in an ephemeral in-memory AttributionContext; NO PERSISTENCE + fires AttributionSuppressedByConsent. Publishes the `currentAttribution` + `currentAttributionId` + `attributionSnapshot` macros on the Request class. Runs AFTER auth (needs the authenticated User to identify) but BEFORE any domain writes (so downstream code sees the resolved attribution).
middleware.notes: Auto-applied globally when the tenant's attribution_capture entitlement is on. The middleware short-circuits when the entitlement is off — same behaviour as the feature-flags attribution.middleware kill-switch.

middleware.description: Reads attribution_id or (subject_type, subject_id) from the request body (admin routes: merge, reset, import) or the X-Attribution-Id header. Loads the Attribution model, checks tenant scoping (refuses when attribution.tenant_id != request tenant with ATTRIBUTION_RESET_TENANT_MISMATCH), and stores it on RequestContext::attributionSubject() so admin actions operate on the resolved profile. Optional (skipped when the request carries no attribution reference — most read routes).

consumed.note: Populates the ConsentGate state that AttributionMiddleware checks.
The permission-based authorize middleware wraps the ability checks defined in policies.json + permissions.json.
ResolveScope narrows admin read queries by the caller's active branch/organization scope. Attribution profiles cascade through tenant only (no branch/org scoping).
